title: Earthy Fluffy Rice Stir-fry
description: This low-sodium-friendly meal-prep Filipino dish features earthy flavors with a touch of Chinese sesame sauce and is perfect for breakfast or lunch.

ingredients.name: Broccoli florets
ingredients.notes: Wet broccoli can be used instead for added moisture and nutrients

ingredients.name: Garlic cloves
ingredients.notes: Chopped finely, optional for extra flavor

ingredients.name: Tofu cubes
ingredients.notes: Replace with tempeh for a complete protein option

ingredients.name: Vegetable broth or water
ingredients.notes: Use for deglazing the pan after stir-frying

ingredients.name: Chinese sesame oil
ingredients.notes: For added flavor and to prevent sticking

ingredients.name: Low-sodium fish sauce or miso paste (optional)
ingredients.notes: To enhance umami and balance flavors, optional for low sodium version

instructions: Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat. Add sesame oil and let it coat the pan.
Add tofu cubes and cook until lightly browned on all sides. Remove from pan and set aside.
Deglaze the pan with vegetable broth or water, scraping up any bits stuck to the bottom.
Reduce heat to low. Add chopped garlic and scallions; sauté for 2-3 minutes.
Add broccoli florets, cover with a lid if your pan allows it, and cook until tender but still crisp-tender, about 5 minutes.
Return tofu cubes to the skillet along with any juices and add soy sauce. Stir well to combine all ingredients.
Serve hot over cooked basmati rice.
